text:
Market today: Micron's blowout earnings are lighting up chip/semi stocks in premarket — that's the real driver today. Positive read-through for your semi-heavy book, but it's one print; don't let the mood mask your real problems.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest concern — down 12.77% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% with a TRIM signal — size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo, trimming buys you breathing room. SPAL is down 14.48% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF, meaning decay eats you alive if it chops — treat that stop at $19 seriously. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record; margin amplifies any sudden move against you.

Watch: (1) How semis react once the regular session opens — Micron's pop could lift SMH and AVGO, giving you a better trim price on AVGO. (2) Iran/Strait of Hormuz headline — energy shock can spike inflation fears and hit growth/tech names hard.

Bottom line: Use any semi rally today to reduce your two biggest losing positions — PLTR and AVGO — before margin forces the decision for you.
